---
title: "Bobtail_activity"
author: "DEKERLE_Louis"
date: "2026-05-01"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r LIBRARIES}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(lubridate)
```

```{r DATA}

data_bob = read.csv("metadata.csv", sep =";", header = TRUE)


```

```{r sum of observations per day}

# create a column with "1" everywhere to be able to sum le lines 
data_bob$count = 1 

# plot
ggplot(data_bob, aes(x = date, y = count)) +
  geom_col()
```

```{r sum of observations per hour}

# create a column with "1" everywhere to be able to sum le lines 
data_bob$count = 1 


data_bob$time = as.POSIXct(data_bob$time, format = "%H:%M")
data_bob$hour = hour(data_bob$time)

# plot  
ggplot(data_bob, aes(x = time, y = count)) +
  geom_col() +
  xlab("Time of trapping") +
  ylab("Number of bobtails on all pictures")

# plot  
ggplot(data_bob, aes(x = hour, y = count)) +
  geom_col() +
  xlab("Hour of trapping") +
  ylab("Number of bobtails on all pictures")
```

```{r sum of observations across camera temperatures}

# create a column with "1" everywhere to be able to sum le lines 
data_bob$count = 1 

# plot  
ggplot(data_bob, aes(x = temperature, y = count)) +
  geom_col() +
  xlab("Camera temperature") +
  ylab("Number of bobtails on all pictures")

```

```{r sum of observations per individuals}

# create a column with "1" everywhere to be able to sum le lines 
data_bob$count = 1 

# plot  
ggplot(data_bob, aes(x = individual, y = count)) +
  geom_col() +
  xlab("Individual") +
  ylab("Number of bobtails on all pictures")

```

```{r sum of observations per camera}

# create a column with "1" everywhere to be able to sum le lines 
data_bob$count = 1 

# plot  
ggplot(data_bob, aes(x = camera, y = count)) +
  geom_col() +
  xlab("camera") +
  ylab("Number of bobtails on all pictures")

```

```{r usm of observation per individual and camera}

# create a column with "1" everywhere to be able to sum le lines 
data_bob$count = 1 

ggplot(data_bob, aes(x = individual, y = count, fill = camera)) +
  geom_col() +
  xlab("Individual") +
  ylab("Number of bobtails on all pictures")
```
```{r sum of observations per max air temperature each day}

# create a column with "1" everywhere to be able to sum le lines 
data_bob$count = 1 

# plot 
ggplot(data_bob, aes(x = max_temp_atmo, y = count)) +
  geom_col() +
  xlab("Max air temperatures each day") +
  ylab("Number of bobtails on all pictures")

```
